bead.title: DESIGN: structured cross-repo blocker ref + auto-recheck
bead.description:
PROBLEM
When a bead in one repo is blocked on work in ANOTHER repo (e.g. niflheim beads blocked on heimq), operators hand-carry 'SKIP these heimq-blocked beads' as prose across sessions and compactions. They wanted to mark them blocked but had to fall back to priority-4 + notes.

CURRENT STATE (from adversarial review — gap is NARROWER than first thought)
- An external-blocker primitive ALREADY exists: cli/internal/bead/lifecycle.go has StatusBlocked + a required ExternalBlockerReason, a LifecycleBucketBlockedExternal bucket, an ExternalBlocked() store query (store.go), and a manual recheck workflow (operator reopens when cleared).
- The RESIDUAL gap: the blocker reason is FREE TEXT with MANUAL reopen. There is no STRUCTURED cross-repo reference (<repo>#<bead>) and no AUTOMATED recheck.

WHAT TO PRODUCE (design, not implementation)
- A short design doc specifying: a structured cross-repo blocker reference field (repo + bead id) layered on the existing ExternalBlockerReason, and an automated recheck that flips the bead back to open when the referenced bead closes. Cover: how the recheck observes another repo's tracker (the referenced repo may be local or remote), failure modes, and backward compatibility with free-text reasons.
- Decompose into child build beads with command-based ACs depending on this epic.

NON-SCOPE (for THIS bead)
- No implementation in this bead — design + child beads only.
- Do not replace the existing external-blocker primitive; build ON it.
- No new lifecycle statuses.
bead.acceptance: 1. A committed design doc exists describing the structured <repo>#<bead> reference + auto-recheck layered on the existing external-blocker primitive. 2. At least one child build bead is created with command-based ACs and wired via ddx bead dep add <child> <this-epic>. 3. ddx bead dep tree <this-epic> shows the children.
